studyName: Ecological Stressors, PTSD, and Drug Use in Detroit: The Detroit Neighborhood Health Study (DNHS)

tables.description: This subject phenotypes table includes socio-demographic information,  general and family health information, mental health data and post-traumatic stress symptoms, PTSD scoring criteria, and history of traumatic experiences over multiple timepoints.

variables.name: Age
variables.description: Age

variables.name: Bcell
variables.description: B lymphocyte cell levels

variables.name: CD4T
variables.description: CD4 T lymphocyte cell levels

variables.name: CD8T
variables.description: Cytotoxic T lymphocyte cell level

variables.name: Gender
variables.description: What is your gender?

variables.name: Mono
variables.description: Monocyte cell levels

variables.name: NK
variables.description: Natural killer cell levels

variables.name: Neu
variables.description: Neutrophil cell levels

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample Id

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: TraumaNum
variables.description: Sum of number of trauma events at each wave

variables.name: Wave
variables.description: Wave

variables.name: race3cat
variables.description: How would you describe your racial background? (Final race with 3 categories)

variables.name: race6cat
variables.description: How would you describe your racial background? (Final race with 6 categories)

variables.name: w1_age_final
variables.description: How old are you?

variables.name: w1_educ
variables.description: What is the highest level of education or schooling you have completed?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_21
variables.description: Have you had a parent who had a problem with drugs or alcohol?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_23
variables.description: Have you been through a divorce or break up with a partner or significant other?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_24
variables.description: Have you lost your job?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_25
variables.description: Have you been emotionally mistreated- for example, shamed, embarrassed, ignored, or repeatedly told you were no good?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_27
variables.description: Have you experienced stressful legal problems- for example, being sued or suing someone else?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_28
variables.description: Have you been unemployed and seeking employment for at least 3 months?

variables.name: w1c1_L1_29
variables.description: Have you had serious financial problems?

variables.name: w1c1_PTSDlife
variables.description: Participant met all six PTSD criteria A-F in lifetime from either worst or random (excluding worst) event in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_PTSDpm
variables.description: Participant met all six PTSD criteria A-F due to either the worst or random (excluding worst) event and most recently experienced the related symptoms in the PAST MONTH

variables.name: w1c1_PTSDpy
variables.description: Participant met all six PTSD criteria A-F due to either the worst or random (excluding worst) event and most recently experienced the related symptoms in the PAST YEAR

variables.name: w1c1_gad7cat
variables.description: Categories of lifetime GAD severity

variables.name: w1c1_life_sumptsdworst
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score based on summing scores for 17 symptoms  (M6-M22) from the worst lifetime trauma.

variables.name: w1c1_life_worst_avoidance
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst avoidance (M11-M17) from the worst lifetime trauma.

variables.name: w1c1_life_worst_hyperarousal
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to hyperarousal (M18-M22) from the worst lifetime trauma.

variables.name: w1c1_life_worst_intrusion
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst intrusion (M6-M10) from the worst lifetime trauma.

variables.name: w1c1_phq9cat
variables.description: Categories of lifetime depression severity: (1) phq9sum=0 (2) phq9sum 1-4 (3) phq9sum 5-9 (4) phq9sum 10-14 (5) phq9sum 15-19 (6) phq9sum 20-27

variables.name: w1c1_stress10
variables.description: Experienced Financial Problems since last interview

variables.name: w1c1_stress2
variables.description: Experienced Parent Problem with Drugs or Alcohol in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_stress4
variables.description: Experienced Divorce or Break Up in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_stress5
variables.description: Experienced Lost Job in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_stress6
variables.description: Experienced Emotionally Mistreated in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_stress8
variables.description: Experienced Legal Problems in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_stress9
variables.description: Experienced Unemployed for at least 3 months in lifetime

variables.name: w1c1_traumanum
variables.description: Number of traumatic events in lifetime

variables.description: How old are you?

variables.name: w2c1_PTSDlife
variables.description: The participant met all six criteria A-F for PTSD based on any selected event, and the related symptoms occurred at any time during their life.  Selected events include: all events for which symptoms were assessed in any wave up to the current wave

variables.name: w2c1_PTSDpm
variables.description: The participant met all six criteria A-F for PTSD based on any selected event, and the related symptoms occurred at any time during the past month. Selected events include: the worst incident trauma identified in the previous interview and the worst incident trauma since the previous interview.

variables.name: w2c1_PTSDpy
variables.description: The participant met all six criteria A-F for PTSD based on any selected event, and the related symptoms occurred at any time during the past year.  Selected events include: the worst incident trauma identified in the previous interview and the worst incident trauma since the previous interview.

variables.name: w2c1_gad7cat_py
variables.description: Categories of GAD (since last interview) severity: (1) gad7sum 5-9 (2) gad7sum 10-14 (3) gad7sum ≥ 15

variables.name: w2c1_inc_sumptsdworst
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score based on summing scores for 17 symptoms  (M6-M22) from the worst incident trauma since the last interview.

variables.name: w2c1_inc_worst_avoidance
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst avoidance (M11-M17) from the worst incident trauma since the last interview.

variables.name: w2c1_inc_worst_hyperarousal
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to hyperarousal (M18-M22) from the worst incident trauma since the last interview

variables.name: w2c1_inc_worst_intrusion
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst intrusion (M6-M10) from the worst incident trauma since the last interview

variables.name: w2c1_life_sumptsdworst
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score based on summing scores for 17 symptoms  (M6-M22) from the worst incident trauma since the last interview.

variables.name: w2c1_pyphq9cat
variables.description: Categories of lifetime depression severity: (1) phq9sum=0 (2) phq9sum 1-4 (3) phq9sum 5-9 (4) phq9sum 10-14 (5) phq9sum 15-19 (6) phq9sum 20-27

variables.name: w2c1_r_sumptsdworst
variables.description: Lifetime worst PTS symptom severity based on: 1) summing scores for 17 symptoms for each of the worst traumas identified in previous interviews and the current interview. 2) choosing the largest score from 1

variables.name: w2c1_r_worst_avoidance
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst avoidance (JJ6-JJ12).

variables.name: w2c1_r_worst_hyperarousal
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to hyperarousal (JJ13-JJ17).

variables.name: w2c1_r_worst_intrusion
variables.description: PTS symptom severity score subset created by summing symptoms related to worst intrusion (JJ1-JJ5).

variables.name: w3_J1
variables.description: How often on a day-to-day basis did you experience each of the following: People act as if you are inferior?

variables.description: People act as if you are not smart?

variables.name: w3_J3
variables.description: People act as if they are afraid of you?

variables.name: w3_J4
variables.description: You are treated with less courtesy than others?

variables.description: You are treated with less respect than others?

variables.name: w3_J6
variables.description: You receive poor service in stores or restaurants?

variables.name: w3_J7
variables.description: People act as if you are dishonest?

variables.name: w3_J8
variables.description: You are called names or insulted?

variables.name: w3_J9
variables.description: You are threatened or harassed?

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table contains data obtained through the use of PhenX variables, and provides information about use of illicit drugs, general health, family health, and socio-demographic information.

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: phx_D17A
variables.description: Did your mother ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D17B
variables.description: Did your father ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D17C
variables.description: Did your sister ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D17D
variables.description: Did your brother ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D17E
variables.description: Did your daughter ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D17F
variables.description: Did your son ever have a heart attack?

variables.name: phx_D18A
variables.description: Age of participant's mother when her first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_D18B
variables.description: Age of participant's father when his first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_D18C
variables.description: Age of participant's sister when her first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_D18D
variables.description: Age of participant's brother when his first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_D18E
variables.description: Age of participant's daughter when her first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_D18F
variables.description: Age of participant's son when his first heart attack occurred

variables.name: phx_DSCORE
variables.description: CREATE A SCORING VARIABLE. Sum the number of unhealthy days from phx_ND2 and phx_ND3.

variables.name: phx_ETHNIC
variables.description: Do you consider yourself Hispanic or Latino?

variables.name: phx_GENDER
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: phx_HISP_1
variables.description: Groups best representing participant's Hispanic origin or ancestry. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #1)

variables.name: phx_ND1
variables.description: Would you say that in general your health is:

variables.name: phx_ND16
variables.description: Did your mother father full-blooded sisters full-blooded brothers daughters or sons ever have a heart attack or myocardial infarction?

variables.name: phx_ND2
variables.description: Now thinking about your physical health which includes physical illness and injury for how many days during the past 30 days was your physical health not good?

variables.name: phx_ND3
variables.description: Now thinking about your mental health which includes stress depression and problems with emotions for how many days during the past 30 days was your mental health not good?

variables.name: phx_ND4
variables.description: During the past 30 days for about how many days did poor physical or mental health keep you from doing your usual activities such as self-care work or recreation?

variables.name: phx_NU1
variables.description: Did participant give date of birth?

variables.name: phx_NU2
variables.description: Current marital status of participant

variables.name: phx_NUU32_
variables.description: Specify health insurance if participant selected: Other

variables.name: phx_RACE_1
variables.description: Race of participant. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #1)

variables.name: phx_RACE_2
variables.description: Race of participant. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #2)

variables.name: phx_RACE_3
variables.description: Race of participant. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #3)

variables.name: phx_RUACE_
variables.description: Specify race further if for phx_RACE_1- phx_RACE_10, the participant answered: Other Pacific Islander, Other Asian, or Some Other Race

variables.name: phx_U25
variables.description: Current educational attainment of participant

variables.name: phx_U30_1
variables.description: Current employment status of participant. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #1)

variables.description: Current employment status of participant. Multiple answers allowed. (SELECTION #2)

variables.name: phx_const_projwts
variables.description: Sampling weights for participants with survey data.  These weights sum up to the Detroit population of adults (18 yrs and older) in 2010 (n=531,551)

variables.name: phx_const_sampwts
variables.description: Sampling weights for participants with survey data.  These weights sum up to the total sample size (n=791)

variables.name: phx_gwas_const_projwts
variables.description: Sampling weights for participants with both survey and GWAS data.  These weights sum up to the Detroit population of adults (18 yrs and older) in 2010 (n=531,551)

variables.name: phx_gwas_const_sampwts
variables.description: Sampling weights for participants with both survey and GWAS data.  These weights sum up to the total sample size (n=473)

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site and analyte type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Sample component used

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes  analyte type, tumor status, months after diagnosis, and treatment description prior to collection.

variables.description: Sample component used
